name: FUNCTION_COMBINATIONS_FOR_TOP_ASSIGNEE_TARGETPAIRS
description: Functional category combinations for top assignee target pairs. Auto-generated from the core-four benchmark and aligned to the validated query-frame Cypher semantics.
cypher:
MATCH (ymax:Year)
WITH max(ymax.year) AS max_year
MATCH (tp:TargetPair)<-[:HAS_TARGET_PAIR]-(p0:Patent)-[:PUBLISHED_IN]->(y0:Year)
WITH tp, min(y0.year) AS first_year, max_year
WHERE first_year >= max_year - $years + 1
MATCH (tp)<-[:HAS_TARGET_PAIR]-(p:Patent)-[:HAS_ASSIGNEE]->(a:Assignee)
WITH a, tp, count(DISTINCT p) AS patent_count
ORDER BY patent_count DESC
WITH a, collect(DISTINCT tp)[0..10] AS tps
UNWIND tps AS tp
MATCH (tp)-[:HAS_TARGET]->(t:Target)-[:FUNCTIONED_AS]->(f:Functional_of_Target)
RETURN a.name AS assignee, tp.name AS target_pair, collect(DISTINCT f.name) AS functional_categories
ORDER BY assignee, target_pair
LIMIT 100

examples: For the novel combinations associated with those highly active companies, what functional-of-target pairings do they correspond to?
Which functional-of-target combination patterns are represented by the novel target pairs linked to those highly active companies?
Among the novel combinations tied to those active companies, what functional categories do the paired targets fall into?
